---
title: "X hangs on logout,shutdown,restart"
date: 2006-03-02
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by adam.stokes on 2006-03-02
Have nvidia module installed X server starts up just fine but when I restart X or logout the system just completely hangs :(


---

### Post by tuke81 on 2006-03-03
Well I had similar problems when I used **Option "NvAGP" "1"** in my xorg.conf. Try to change it to **Option "NvAGP" "2"** or **Option "NvAGP" "3"** If your mobo is one of the [[COLOR="Navy"]nvidia's list[/COLOR]]("http://nvidia.custhelp.com/cgi-bin/nvidia.cfg/php/enduser/std_adp.php?p_faqid=149&p_created=1100107993&p_sid=q*2oFy1i&p_lva=174&p_sp=cF9zcmNoPTEmcF9zb3J0X2J5PSZwX2dyaWRzb3J0PSZwX3Jvd19jbnQ9MTMmcF9wcm9kcz0wJnBfY2F0cz0wJnBfcHY9JnBfY3Y9JnBfc2VhcmNoX3R5cGU9YW5zd2Vycy5zZWFyY2hfZm5sJnBfcGFnZT0xJnBfc2VhcmNoX3RleHQ9QUdQR0FSVA**&p_li=&p_topview=1"), then it might work with setting "NvAGP" "1". If its not use some other setting. :cool:

EDIT: You can test if agp is working by using command **cat /proc/driver/nvidia/agp/status** in terminal.

---

### Post by adam.stokes on 2006-03-03
The interesting thing is not providing Option "NvAGP"  "3" will by default switch back to using the built in kernel agpgart, however, the lockups still continue when trying to log out.

Thanks for your suggestion have any other ideas? :)

In the dmesg below it shows falling back to agpgart, however, not sure why it is trying to load the agp device at busid 00:00.0 and again still leaves me to wonder why the X hangs are happening :(


```
[4294723.003000] NVRM: not using NVAGP, an AGPGART backend is loaded!
[4294723.716000] agpgart: Found an AGP 3.0 compliant device at 0000:00:00.0.
[4294723.716000] agpgart: reserved bits set in mode 0x1f00000c. Fixed.
[4294723.716000] agpgart: Xorg tried to set rate=x0. Setting to AGP3 x4 mode.
[4294723.716000] agpgart: Putting AGP V3 device at 0000:00:00.0 into 4x mode
[4294723.716000] agpgart: Putting AGP V3 device at 0000:01:00.0 into 4x mode
[4294724.460000] NVRM: not using NVAGP, an AGPGART backend is loaded!

```

oh one last thing, even though falling back on agpgart the following is displayed in proc :

```

adam@franky:~$ cat /proc/driver/nvidia/agp/status
Status:          Disabled

AGP initialization failed, please check the ouput
of the 'dmesg' command and/or your system log file
for additional information on this problem.

```

---

### Post by adam.stokes on 2006-03-03
Compiled latest driver for my kernel and it fixed itself :)

---
